extraction.one_line_summary: KRAGH ANDERSEN Søren won from breakaway after CAVANIA Rémi shredded peloton at 31.6mph opening hour, fastest of entire Tour.
tactical_patterns: CAVANIA Rémi produced Tour's fastest opening hour at 31.6mph, weaponizing breakaway specialist role to exhaust rival leadout trains.
BENNETT Sam strategically avoided stage win contention despite being well-positioned, prioritizing green jersey security over additional glory.
Quick Step and Ineos simultaneously attacked early to control intermediate sprint 30 miles from finish, coordinated cross-team tactical move.

rider_signals.KRAGH ANDERSEN Søren: positive: second identical stage win (Lyon rematch with Mozzato second again), Sunweb's young Danish contingent overperforming Tour expectations
rider_signals.CAVANIA Rémi: positive: late Tour addition to Deceuninck roster immediately delivered strategic value through pace-forcing rather than personal results
rider_signals.POSTELBERGER Lukas: negative: bee sting retirement removes Sagan's key leadout man on stage 19, catastrophic timing blow to green jersey challenge
rider_signals.BENNETT Sam: positive: deliberately chose not to contest stage win despite positioning, mature tactical restraint with 50+ point green jersey cushion
rider_signals.SAGAN Peter: negative: losing Postelberger this late eliminates leadout infrastructure when Bennett still vulnerable, team depth gap exposed
